lesson.menu_desc: Review the whole communication block -- emails, reminders, and appointments, all in one -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Communication Checkpoint -- Emails, Reminders & Appointments
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Percival JR | Aula 20 -- Communication Checkpoint

listenings.text: Hi Percival, this is Karen. Before our holiday, let me hand over the supplier thread, so nothing falls through. Three things are open. First, a customer emailed about a late delivery -- please reply, acknowledge the problem, and offer a clear solution. Second, the supplier still owes us the final numbers -- we are still waiting, so send a short, polite reminder and set a deadline for Friday. Third, the contract review is on hold -- once they reply, reach out and arrange a new appointment. Keep everything in writing, stay kind, and follow through on each item. If anything is urgent, call me. You have done great work this whole block. I trust you to wrap it all up. Thank you so much.

Hi Percival, this is Mr. Santos from the supplier. Good news on all three points. First, about the late delivery your customer mentioned -- I have sorted it out, and the replacement ships today. Second, here are the final numbers you have been chasing -- I am sending them now, well before your Friday deadline. Sorry again for the silence. And third, I would love to arrange that contract review. I am free next week, so please reach out and propose a time. You handled this whole thread very professionally, always in writing and always polite. It was a pleasure. I will look into anything else you need. Talk soon.

extra_audio.text: First, Percival replies to the customer about the late delivery and offers a clear solution. Then, he sends the silent supplier a short, polite reminder with a Friday deadline. After that, the supplier finally answers and sends the final numbers. Later, Percival reaches out to arrange the contract review appointment. Finally, he confirms a convenient time, and the whole thread is wrapped up in writing.
